channel.title: Kubectl on blogu geegu.
channel.description: Recent content in Kubectl on blogu geegu.

item.title: Better kubectl interaction
item.description: As many of you know, kubectl is official command-line client for interacting with the kubernetes cluster. Although kubectl allow us to do almost anything with the cluster, it has a convoluted interface. Especially for interacting with basic entities such pods. Pods are ephemeral entities, and each instance gets an unique name. If a deployment name is kafka-consumer then pods names might be kafka-consumer-654b7df6b-mfx46, kafka-consumer-654b7df6b-yy49i. This name changes every time pod is updated or restarted and all commands which needs to interact with pods needs this unique pod name as its parameter.
